reasoning: `lemma_obtain_bit_index_1` is `external_body` — the property that any nonzero usize has a set bit below 64 is mathematically true, but it is trusted without proof. This is a real trust gap in the verification.

`lemma_obtain_bit_index_2` is fully verified (not external_body) — it correctly derives the existence of a clear bit from `lemma_obtain_bit_index_1` applied to `!a`. The property itself is mathematically correct and the proof is sound modulo the existing external_body dependency.

This is a tautology — `is_bit_set` is defined as `a & (1usize << b) == (1usize << b)`, so revealing it and asserting the same expression trivially holds. The `b < 64` guard in the requires makes this a safe, expected usage. The wrapping concern at `b >= 64` is not exercised here.
